title: Rounding the Square Root to the Nearest Integer

statement.markdown: Given a number n, find the square root of n rounded to the nearest integer. If the square root is a half integer, round up.
statement.outputFormat: The square root of the input integer rounded to the nearest integer
statement.notes: The function should return an integer value

examples.explanation: The square root of 25 is 5, which is an integer.
The square root of 26 is not an integer, but rounding up to the nearest integer would make it 5.

hints.text: Use the sqrt function from the math module

editorial.title: Rounding the Square Root to the Nearest Integer
editorial.intuition: You can use the math.sqrt function to calculate the square root of the number.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve calculating the square root of the number and rounding it up or down based on whether it is a half integer.
editorial.betterSolution: You can use the math.ceil function to round up to the nearest integer.
editorial.optimalSolution: The math.ceil function is optimal because it rounds up to the nearest integer, which is what the problem requires.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The math.ceil function is correct because it returns the smallest integer that is greater than or equal to the given number.
editorial.dryRun: If the number is 26, the math.sqrt function will return 5.099, and the math.ceil function will return 6.
